speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: Thus comes the English with full power upon us,

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: And more than carefully it us concerns

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: To answer royally in our defenses.

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: Therefore the Dukes of Berri and of Brittany,

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: Of Brabant and of Orléans, shall make forth,

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: And you, Prince Dauphin, with all swift dispatch,

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: To line and new-repair our towns of war

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: With men of courage and with means defendant.

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: For England his approaches makes as fierce

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: As waters to the sucking of a gulf.

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: It fits us then to be as provident

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: As fear may teach us out of late examples

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: Left by the fatal and neglected English

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: Upon our fields.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: My most redoubted father,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: It is most meet we arm us ’gainst the foe,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: For peace itself should not so dull a kingdom,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Though war nor no known quarrel were in question

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: But that defenses, musters, preparations

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Should be maintained, assembled, and collected

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: As were a war in expectation.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Therefore I say ’tis meet we all go forth

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: To view the sick and feeble parts of France.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: And let us do it with no show of fear,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: No, with no more than if we heard that England

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Were busied with a Whitsun morris-dance.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: For, my good liege, she is so idly kinged,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Her scepter so fantastically borne

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: By a vain, giddy, shallow, humorous youth,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: That fear attends her not.

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: O peace, Prince Dauphin!

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: You are too much mistaken in this king.

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: Question your Grace the late ambassadors

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: With what great state he heard their embassy,

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: How well supplied with noble councillors,

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: How modest in exception, and withal

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: How terrible in constant resolution,

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: And you shall find his vanities forespent

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: Were but the outside of the Roman Brutus,

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: Covering discretion with a coat of folly,

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: As gardeners do with ordure hide those roots

speaker: CONSTABLE
text: That shall first spring and be most delicate.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Well, ’tis not so, my Lord High Constable.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: But though we think it so, it is no matter.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: In cases of defense, ’tis best to weigh

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: The enemy more mighty than he seems.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: So the proportions of defense are filled,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Which of a weak and niggardly projection

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Doth, like a miser, spoil his coat with scanting

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: A little cloth.

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: Think we King Harry strong,

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: And, princes, look you strongly arm to meet him.

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: The kindred of him hath been fleshed upon us,

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: And he is bred out of that bloody strain

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: That haunted us in our familiar paths.

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: Witness our too-much-memorable shame

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: When Cressy battle fatally was struck

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: And all our princes captived by the hand

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: Of that black name, Edward, Black Prince of Wales,

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: Whiles that his mountain sire, on mountain standing

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: Up in the air, crowned with the golden sun,

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: Saw his heroical seed and smiled to see him

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: Mangle the work of nature and deface

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: The patterns that by God and by French fathers

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: Had twenty years been made. This is a stem

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: Of that victorious stock, and let us fear

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: The native mightiness and fate of him.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Ambassadors from Harry King of England

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Do crave admittance to your Majesty.

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: We’ll give them present audience. Go, and bring them.

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: You see this chase is hotly followed, friends.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Turn head and stop pursuit, for coward dogs

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Most spend their mouths when what they seem to threaten

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Runs far before them. Good my sovereign,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Take up the English short, and let them know

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Of what a monarchy you are the head.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Self-love, my liege, is not so vile a sin

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: As self-neglecting.

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: From our brother of England?

speaker: EXETER
text: From him, and thus he greets your Majesty:

speaker: EXETER
text: He wills you, in the name of God almighty,

speaker: EXETER
text: That you divest yourself and lay apart

speaker: EXETER
text: The borrowed glories that, by gift of heaven,

speaker: EXETER
text: By law of nature and of nations, ’longs

speaker: EXETER
text: To him and to his heirs—namely, the crown

speaker: EXETER
text: And all wide-stretchèd honors that pertain

speaker: EXETER
text: By custom and the ordinance of times

speaker: EXETER
text: Unto the crown of France. That you may know

speaker: EXETER
text: ’Tis no sinister nor no awkward claim

speaker: EXETER
text: Picked from the wormholes of long-vanished days

speaker: EXETER
text: Nor from the dust of old oblivion raked,

speaker: EXETER
text: He sends you this most memorable line,

speaker: EXETER
text: In every branch truly demonstrative,

speaker: EXETER
text: Willing you overlook this pedigree,

speaker: EXETER
text: And when you find him evenly derived

speaker: EXETER
text: From his most famed of famous ancestors,

speaker: EXETER
text: Edward the Third, he bids you then resign

speaker: EXETER
text: Your crown and kingdom, indirectly held

speaker: EXETER
text: From him, the native and true challenger.

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: Or else what follows?

speaker: EXETER
text: Bloody constraint, for if you hide the crown

speaker: EXETER
text: Even in your hearts, there will he rake for it.

speaker: EXETER
text: Therefore in fierce tempest is he coming,

speaker: EXETER
text: In thunder and in earthquake like a Jove,

speaker: EXETER
text: That, if requiring fail, he will compel,

speaker: EXETER
text: And bids you, in the bowels of the Lord,

speaker: EXETER
text: Deliver up the crown and to take mercy

speaker: EXETER
text: On the poor souls for whom this hungry war

speaker: EXETER
text: Opens his vasty jaws, and on your head

speaker: EXETER
text: Turning the widows’ tears, the orphans’ cries,

speaker: EXETER
text: The dead men’s blood, the privèd maidens’ groans,

speaker: EXETER
text: For husbands, fathers, and betrothèd lovers

speaker: EXETER
text: That shall be swallowed in this controversy.

speaker: EXETER
text: This is his claim, his threat’ning, and my message—

speaker: EXETER
text: Unless the Dauphin be in presence here,

speaker: EXETER
text: To whom expressly I bring greeting too.

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: For us, we will consider of this further.

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: Tomorrow shall you bear our full intent

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: Back to our brother of England.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: For the Dauphin,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: I stand here for him. What to him from England?

speaker: EXETER
text: Scorn and defiance, slight regard, contempt,

speaker: EXETER
text: And anything that may not misbecome

speaker: EXETER
text: The mighty sender, doth he prize you at.

speaker: EXETER
text: Thus says my king: an if your father’s Highness

speaker: EXETER
text: Do not, in grant of all demands at large,

speaker: EXETER
text: Sweeten the bitter mock you sent his Majesty,

speaker: EXETER
text: He’ll call you to so hot an answer of it

speaker: EXETER
text: That caves and womby vaultages of France

speaker: EXETER
text: Shall chide your trespass and return your mock

speaker: EXETER
text: In second accent of his ordinance.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Say, if my father render fair return,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: It is against my will, for I desire

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Nothing but odds with England. To that end,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: As matching to his youth and vanity,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: I did present him with the Paris balls.

speaker: EXETER
text: He’ll make your Paris Louvre shake for it,

speaker: EXETER
text: Were it the mistress court of mighty Europe.

speaker: EXETER
text: And be assured you’ll find a difference,

speaker: EXETER
text: As we his subjects have in wonder found,

speaker: EXETER
text: Between the promise of his greener days

speaker: EXETER
text: And these he masters now. Now he weighs time

speaker: EXETER
text: Even to the utmost grain. That you shall read

speaker: EXETER
text: In your own losses, if he stay in France.

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: Tomorrow shall you know our mind at full.

speaker: EXETER
text: Dispatch us with all speed, lest that our king

speaker: EXETER
text: Come here himself to question our delay,

speaker: EXETER
text: For he is footed in this land already.

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: You shall be soon dispatched with fair conditions.

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: A night is but small breath and little pause

speaker:
KING
               
              OF
               
              FRANCE
text: To answer matters of this consequence.
